Politics.headline: FBI raids Virginia state senator's office in possible corruption probe
Politics.detail: The FBI raided the office of a Virginia state senator in a possible corruption probe. The investigation is ongoing, and details are still emerging.

Politics.headline: Court releases purported suicide note from Jeffrey Epstein
Politics.detail: A note discovered by Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate, purported to be a suicide note, has been released by the court after being under seal for years. However, no court or investigative agency has verified its authenticity.

Politics.headline: Four killed in post-election violence in India's West Bengal
Politics.detail: Following recent elections, violence in West Bengal, India has resulted in the deaths of four people. The situation remains tense in the region.

Politics.headline: Trump's mandatory detention policy fails in Florida appeals court
Politics.detail: A federal appeals court in Florida struck down the Trump administration's mandatory detention policy. The ruling is the latest legal challenge to the policy of holding people in immigration detention without the possibility of bond.

Politics.headline: UCLA medical school illegally used race in admissions, justice department finds
Politics.detail: The Justice Department has found that the UCLA medical school illegally used race in its admissions process. Further details about the findings are expected to be released.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's total exports hit record $863 billion in FY26, services surge 8.7%
Finance & Economy.detail: India's total exports reached a record high of $863 billion in fiscal year 2026, with services experiencing a significant surge of 8.7%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Four in five Britons worried Iran war will make food more expensive, poll finds
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent poll indicates that 80% of Britons are concerned that a potential war involving Iran could lead to increased food prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: Foreclosure filings hit 6-year high as costs strain American homeowners
Finance & Economy.detail: The number of foreclosure filings in the United States has reached a six-year high, signaling increasing financial strain on homeowners due to rising costs.

Finance & Economy.headline: Market Outlook: AI optimism pushes S&P 500 to fresh record highs
Finance & Economy.detail: Optimism surrounding artificial intelligence (AI) is driving the S&P 500 to new record highs, reflecting investor confidence in the sector's growth potential.

Finance & Economy.headline: Honda more definitively halting $15B EV plant development in Ontario, according to reports
Finance & Economy.detail: Reports suggest that Honda Motor is halting the development of its $15 billion electric vehicle complex in Ontario, although Honda Canada has not confirmed this.

Technology.headline: Starlink Drops Price As Low As $29/Month to Win Over New Users
Technology.detail: Starlink is offering a discount that lowers the monthly price to $29 for the first three months of a Residential subscription, aiming to attract new users.

Technology.headline: Gamers Struggle to Buy New Steam Controller, Valve Pledges Restock 'Soon'
Technology.detail: The new Steam Controller quickly sold out after orders went live, with scalpers listing the $99 device for up to $700 on eBay; Valve has promised a restock soon.

Technology.headline: Utah Takes Aim at VPNs With New Age-Verification Law
Technology.detail: A new law in Utah targets minors using VPNs to access porn sites, requiring age verification, but critics worry about unintended consequences due to its vague wording.

Technology.headline: Your Xbox Won't Get Microsoft Copilot AI Features After All
Technology.detail: Microsoft's Copilot AI features will not be coming to Xbox, as confirmed by Xbox's new CEO, stating that it doesn't align with the brand's direction.

Technology.headline: Genetic 'bonus material' boosts gut bacterium's oxygen tolerance up to 1,000-fold
Technology.detail: Recent research has uncovered how genetic 'bonus material' can significantly enhance a gut bacterium's ability to tolerate oxygen.

Science.headline: Controlled peanut intake may reduce allergies in toddlers
Science.detail: Researchers at Karolinska Institutet have successfully treated children aged 1–3 years with peanut allergies by gradually increasing their daily peanut consumption, desensitizing their immune systems. All children who followed the protocol achieved the goal of eating three and a half peanuts without experiencing an allergic reaction, and most were able to consume up to 25 peanuts.

Science.headline: Microsoft's Climate Goals Under AI Pressure
Science.detail: Microsoft is reportedly considering delaying or abandoning its 2030 goal of matching its entire hourly electricity use with renewable energy purchases because the expensive and energy-intensive push for data centers is reshaping the feasibility of Microsoft's climate commitments that were made before the AI era. Like rivals Amazon and Alphabet, the Windows maker is spending hundreds of billions of dollars to build out artificial intelligence infrastructure needed to power services such as its Copilot assistant and Azure cloud service.

Science.headline: Dynamic catalyst interfaces offer a smarter route for converting CO₂ into formic acid
Science.detail: A new study explores dynamic catalyst interfaces as a smarter way to convert CO₂ into formic acid. This approach may offer a more efficient and sustainable method for producing this valuable chemical.

Science.headline: Human language shows deep safety bias, challenging 70-year scientific consensus
Science.detail: A recent study challenges a 70-year scientific consensus by revealing a deep safety bias in human language. This finding suggests that our communication is inherently geared towards prioritizing safety and avoiding potential threats.

Science.headline: New long-necked dinosaur found in Northeast Brazil was a close relative of a European species.
Science.detail: A newly discovered long-necked dinosaur in Northeast Brazil has been identified as a close relative of a European species. This finding provides insights into the connections between dinosaur populations across different continents.

Health.headline: International Health Alert: Hantavirus Cases on Cruise Ship Rise to Five
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has announced an increase in confirmed Hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship, bringing the total to five, and has raised its alert level to address this limited outbreak. Two additional passengers were evacuated for treatment at specialized medical centers amid strict precautionary measures aimed at preventing the ship from becoming an epidemic hotspot.

Health.headline: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship: 3 Dead, Patients Evacuated to Europe
Health.detail: Three people have died and eight cases have been recorded in all due to a Hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, according to the World Health Organization. Contact tracing has begun in Europe and Africa in search of infections around people who earlier left the ship, which departed over a month ago from South America.

Health.headline: Three evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship
Health.detail: Emergency crews evacuated three people from a cruise ship stricken with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus, the WHO said, as experts confirmed a rare strain that can be transmitted between humans. Two crew members and one other person thought to be infected were being taken off the MV Hondius and will be flown to the Netherlands for treatment.

Health.headline: Government of Canada to make an announcement on mental health support
Health.detail: The Honourable Eleanor Olszewski, Minister of Emergency Management and Community Resilience and Minister responsible for Prairies Economic Development Canada (PrairiesCan), on behalf of the Honourable Marjorie Michel, Minister of Health, will make an announcement on mental health and psychosocial supports in emergency settings on May 7, 2026. There will be a media availability immediately following the announcement.

Health.headline: Mandaviya to launch nationwide free Annual Health Check-Up Initiative
Health.detail: Union Minister of Labour & Employment Mansukh Mandaviya will launch a nationwide free 'Annual Health Check-Up Initiative' for Workers aged 40 years and above on May 7, 2026, as proposed under the new Labour Codes. The initiative will be formally inaugurated from the Employees' State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) Medical College & Hospital, Basaidarapur, New Delhi, with simultaneous launch events across 11 other ESIC hospitals.

Sports.headline: PSG hold off Bayern to book final showdown with Arsenal
Sports.detail: Paris Saint-Germain advanced to the Champions League final after a 1-1 draw against Bayern Munich, setting up a final match against Arsenal. The match was tightly contested, with PSG ultimately securing their spot on aggregate.

Sports.headline: Man City clinches Women's Super League title to end Chelsea's dominance
Sports.detail: Manchester City has won the Women's Super League title, bringing an end to Chelsea's recent dominance in the league. The victory marks a significant achievement for Man City and a shift in the landscape of women's soccer in England.

Sports.headline: Runner dies after medical emergency during 253-mile ultramarathon
Sports.detail: A runner tragically passed away after experiencing a medical emergency during a 253-mile ultramarathon in Arizona. The incident highlights the extreme challenges and risks associated with such endurance events.

Sports.headline: Joel Embiid is out for Game 2 of the 76ers' series against the Knicks with ankle and hip injuries
Sports.detail: Joel Embiid will miss Game 2 of the 76ers' series against the Knicks due to ankle and hip injuries. This absence is a significant blow to the 76ers' chances as they face a tough opponent in the playoffs.

Sports.headline: Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo is not running in the Preakness
Sports.detail: Golden Tempo, the winner of the Kentucky Derby, will not be participating in the Preakness Stakes. The decision alters the landscape of the race and opens up opportunities for other contenders.

Entertainment.headline: Ted Turner Dies: CNN Founder Dead at 87
Entertainment.detail: Media mogul and CNN founder, Ted Turner, has passed away at the age of 87. Turner transformed the media landscape and was a global media titan.

Entertainment.headline: Matthew Perry's belongings to be auctioned as Friends memorabilia and original Banksy up for sale
Entertainment.detail: Items belonging to the late actor Matthew Perry, including Friends memorabilia, are going up for auction. The auction also features an original Banksy artwork.

Entertainment.headline: Blake Lively Emails Show Spiraling Over 'It Ends With Us' Backlash
Entertainment.detail: Emails reveal Blake Lively's distress over the backlash surrounding the film adaptation of 'It Ends With Us'. The dispute between Lively and Justin Baldoni has been settled.

Entertainment.headline: Rihanna Looks Unhappy as A$AP Rocky Chats With Woman at Met Gala
Entertainment.detail: Photos suggest Rihanna appeared unhappy while A$AP Rocky was conversing with another woman at the Met Gala. This sparked rumors and speculation.

Entertainment.headline: Bill Cosby Found Liable in Sexual Assault Case, Owes Victim $59M
Entertainment.detail: Bill Cosby has been found liable in a sexual assault case and ordered to pay $59 million in damages to Donna Motsinger for drugging and assaulting her in 1972.

Crime & Justice.headline: Child protection workers stood down after Alice Springs girl's death
Crime & Justice.detail: Three child protection workers have been stood down after it was revealed multiple welfare reports were raised about a young girl before her alleged murder in Alice Springs, Australia; a man has been charged with the 5-year-old's murder, spurring riots and calls for a review of conditions within town camps.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect arrested in Oklahoma party shooting that killed 1, injured 22
Crime & Justice.detail: Police have arrested an 18-year-old in connection with a shooting at a party in Oklahoma that resulted in one death and 22 injuries.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Announces Results of Operation Iron Pursuit
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department announced the results of Operation Iron Pursuit.

Crime & Justice.headline: Correspondents' dinner suspect faces new charge of assaulting federal officer
Crime & Justice.detail: Cole Allen, who is accused of targeting Trump, faces a revised indictment alleging he assaulted a federal officer with a deadly weapon.

Crime & Justice.headline: Palm Beach County Sheriff's deputy behind bars accused of kidnapping and other crimes
Crime & Justice.detail: A Palm Beach County Sheriff's deputy has been arrested and is accused of kidnapping, while two other members of the sheriff's office were also arrested on fraud charges.

Education.headline: Schools must comply with existing safety guidelines to strengthen school safety standards, says Education Ministry
Education.detail: The Education Ministry in Malaysia expressed concern over recent accidents in schools and stated that all schools must comply with the existing Safe School Management Guidelines currently in force. The statement follows recent fatal crash incidents at schools, highlighting the need to strengthen school safety.

Education.headline: How These Schools Doubled Teacher Planning Time
Education.detail: Alliance charter network schools have doubled daily teacher planning time to 8-12 hours per week, which has improved teacher retention and student performance. Chronic absenteeism fell 2% and student performance grew nearly 9% in English/language arts and by more than 36% in math in the pilot schools.

Education.headline: Tomorrow's Teachers Sign On to Shape the Future
Education.detail: College and high school students in Connecticut were celebrated for their choice to enter the teaching profession at a Signing Day and Apprenticeship Graduation for Educators Rising event. The program is supported by the Connecticut State Department of Education, Department of Labor, Connecticut Education Association, and other state agencies and organizations, aiming to nurture the love of teaching early and support students through major milestones.

Education.headline: Palestinian ambassador protests to Foreign Office over 'erasure' by British Museum
Education.detail: The Palestinian ambassador has formally protested to the Foreign Office regarding perceived 'erasure' by the British Museum. Details of the specific issue prompting the protest were not elaborated upon in the search results.

Education.headline: Universities unite behind Nepal's “10k int'l student” ambition
Education.detail: Nepalese universities are collaborating to attract international students, aiming to increase enrollment more than sixfold within three years. This initiative seeks to position Nepal as an "emerging South Asian education destination".

Lifestyle.headline: 7 beloved recipes to cherish for Mother's Day and beyond
Lifestyle.detail: Food is an important part of what many of us hope to pass on, for Mother's Day and beyond.

Lifestyle.headline: My teen pushed me because she wants an iPhone. Do we give in and get her one?
Lifestyle.detail: A parent asks for advice on whether to give in to their teen's demands for an iPhone after the teen physically pushed them.

Lifestyle.headline: A promising new depression therapy focuses on finding paths to joy.
Lifestyle.detail: A new therapy for depression is being explored that emphasizes finding paths to joy.

Lifestyle.headline: Costco is adding chicken strips to the Food Court.
Lifestyle.detail: Costco is expanding its food court menu with the addition of chicken strips.

Lifestyle.headline: German Shepherd, abandoned at a California Walmart with all of his toys, gets a second chance.
Lifestyle.detail: A German Shepherd left at a Walmart in California with his toys is getting a new home.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bayside Editorial: Berlin must deal with lead pipes issue now
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Berlin town officials must continue their pursuit of the water line replacement project along Williams Street to prevent lead from leaching into drinking water, even with funding challenges.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL - Inflationary
Opinion/Editorial.detail: All surveys have consistently shown inflation as the top concern of Filipinos, and President Marcos is getting the lowest marks in tackling the problem as inflation surged to 7.2 percent in April.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — May 7, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Congress party's move to support the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is troubling as it will undermine the INDIA bloc alliance, ignoring the political understanding that held the alliance together. The TVK's rise signals a public desire for political renewal, but its next steps will define its credibility.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Tardy justice
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the slow progress of justice in a case involving allegations against former President Rodrigo Duterte, highlighting concerns about delays in the legal process.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Preparing people to prevent fraud
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial emphasizes the importance of public education and awareness to combat fraud in Taiwan, as criminals are often undeterred by long prison sentences. The government has launched anti-fraud campaigns using various media to highlight common scam tactics.

Zimbabwe.headline: GWEKWERERE CONFIRMS BEATING HIS WIFE NATASHA AFTER ROW OVER A LOVER
Zimbabwe.detail: Details are scarce, but B-Metro reports that Gwekwerere has confirmed physically assaulting his wife, Natasha, following a dispute regarding a lover.

Zimbabwe.headline: CHIVAYO EXCLUSIVE: A SCOOP FOR ZIMPAPERS . . . A refreshing change from the Wild West of podcasts
Zimbabwe.detail: B-Metro touts an exclusive interview with Wicknell Chivayo as a major win for traditional media, contrasting it with the podcast landscape.

Zimbabwe.headline: EDITORIAL COMMENT: CHIVAYO EXCLUSIVE INTERVEW: A MASSIVE COUP FOR TRADITIONAL MEDIA
Zimbabwe.detail: B-Metro editorializes on their exclusive interview with Wicknell Chivayo, calling it a major coup for traditional media.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'WE'RE VERY SORRY, BOSS' . . . CAPS players apologise for Chiefs loss, turn attention to 'Derby De Bus'
Zimbabwe.detail: CAPS United players have apologized for their loss to Chiefs and are now focusing on the 'Derby De Bus,' according to B-Metro.

Zimbabwe.headline: Voice Calls Hit 5.07 Billion Minutes in Zimbabwe as Usage Surges
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe experienced a significant increase in mobile voice usage during the final quarter of 2025, with users spending over 5 billion minutes on calls, despite the global trend towards instant messaging. The Postal and Telecommunications Regulatory Authority of Zimbabwe (POTRAZ) attributes this rise to the growing popularity of affordable on-net bundles and promotional offers.
